figures.caption: FIG. 4. (a) Typical MTRM(t) at T = 23.75 K and H = 40 Oe with the fit in D = 3 using Eq. 7 and the fit in D = 2 using Eq. 8 overlay. The fit in D = 3 starts at t = 0 and ends at t = tco; the fit in D = 2 starts at t = 3 × 104 s and ends at our longest measurement time. (b) The temperature dependence of the growth rate T/(TgZc) in D = 3 and 1/Zc in D = 2. The red dashed line shows the theoretical growth rate parameter Zc in D = 2 with Zc ≈ 7.14 [19].

FIG. 3. αf -adjusted (a) MTRM(t, T ) at short time scales and (b) MTRM(t − tco, T ) at long time scales for different measurement temperatures. The data size is reduced to (a) 4000 points and (b) 1000 points over the entire measurement time using cubic spline interpolation to better demonstrate the differences as a function of temperature.

FIG. 2. (a) Typical MZFC(t) and MFC(t) at T = 23.75 K and H = 40 Oe. (b) Typical MTRM(t) at T = 23.75 K and H = 40 Oe, obtained through MTRM(t) = αfMFC(t)−MZFC(t) with αf = 1.000. The calculated tco = 5.83 × 103 s at T = 23.75 K is labeled in panel (b).

FIG. 5. MZFC(t, T ) and MFC(t, T ) at (a) Tm = 23.00 K, (b) Tm = 23.50 K, and (c) Tm = 23.75 K for L = 4.5 nm CuMn/Cu multilayer sample with H = 40 Oe. The data of MZFC(t, T ) and MFC(t, T ) is reduced to 1000 points using cubic spline interpolation to better demonstrate the differences at different temperatures.
